title: Psilogramme cheilanthoides
intro: Psilogramme cheilanthoides, commonly known as the fern Psilogramme, is a type of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae, characterized by its delicate fronds and creeping rhizome. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Psilogramme cheilanthoides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional Chinese and African herbal medicine.
taxonomy: Psilogramme cheilanthoides is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. It is native to South Africa. This species is classified as being part of the genus Psilogramme, which is a group of plants that are known to have fern-like characteristics despite being flowering plants. The classification of Psilogramme cheilanthoides is further supported by its botanical name, which indicates its relationship to the genus and family. The specific classification of Psilogramme cheilanthoides is still subject to ongoing research and classification updates.
